A person intelligent enough about a lot of things to be able to move off that script a little bit, coming back to it. Any person who is intelligent enough to be intellectually curious to actually want to know, not just ask a question and then wait to ask question number four on the list, sort of not mattering what Dave says has his answer, but to be actually curious about what this person does, what makes them tick, what their attitudes are. So again, as a bonus that you get with this system, Nick, you put in here some really tremendous scripts that you've written for your guys, everything from upselling to thank-you calls. So everything, I guess, is, you've scripted everything with your team. We try to script everything. It goes back to what Dan says, you know, you hang around with animals long enough to know realize you need to tame them. And so you need to- That was a much more elegant way of saying it, by the way. I like this way, I like this way. But I think you have to script it. And I think, because what we've talked about, the pros will make it their own, right? They'll get to the core and the nuances of it, but they will, they'll put their own words and their own language and their own voice inflection, but it really needs to be scripted. We wouldn't go see a movie when everybody's just talking. You know, I mean, Larry David was the only one to do that successfully on HBO other than that. You really want everything scripted. You want them to own the script and we really take a lot of time and we tweak it. Because every morning we have a stand-up and we have some programs to talk a lot about that and teach people how to do that. But we really say, what are we hearing? You know, and we- What's working in the scripts? What do we need to tweak? What needs to be motivated? And sometimes you'll go down a path and the script will be bringing you one thing, but you need to make a quick adjustment because there's something more pressing. And so you want to get that to the front of the script and move it along. Fantastic. And again, they're right in here, so you just got to check that out. Make sure when you get your program that you check that out. Okay, because those are scripts that they can model. Sure. All right. So we're finally going to get- those are some of the foundations. Let's go to the NoBS Six Step System, Dan. And we're going to go into great depth on each one of these steps, except for the last step during our time in this program. But we'll talk about how we're going to get to the last step. Let's just go over each one of them briefly, and then we'll go deep into them later on. The first thing is permission to sell. What is permission to sell? So you can sell without permission. It's sort of in the rape category. And it has- it usually has negative consequences, just like that does. And principally, if not arrest and imprisonment, banishment. So if you are selling by true force without permission, without the willing cooperation and participation of the buyer, you will- if you do it in a community of any kind where there is word of mouth, you will soon be banished, even by people who are satisfied with what they bought, because the process is so ugly that they will talk about the process, not about their happiness and satisfaction with what they bought. And you will certainly- even if you're not banished from a community, you will sort of be prohibited from any after the transaction value in that relationship. Somebody may buy that way, but they're certainly not going to refer anybody to you and put them through that same process. They're really not going to want to buy anything else from you again, even if what they bought was just fine. So it is better to have a cooperating and willing participant. And that starts with permission to start selling. So that's as simple as, Nick, do you have time for this conversation now? Or if not, let's just book a time to get together for lunch next week. That's as simple as- to more complicated, but permission to start selling at the very start of the selling, to permission for almost every sort of benchmark along the way. So my tailor- so the stage stuff I have custom made, the other stuff I don't, because I don't care, but the stage stuff I do.
